questionHtml: Explain, referring to the Maxwell–Boltzmann distribution curve, the effect of a catalyst on a chemical reaction.
markschemeHtml:
increases rate AND there is a greater area under the curve past activation energy

OR

increases rate AND greater proportion of/more molecules have «kinetic» E ≥ Ea «(cat) than Ea (uncat)» ✔

 

Do not award a mark for general statements about catalysts such as “provides alternative pathway” or “lowers Ea”.

meta.title: Explain, referring to the Maxwell–Boltzmann distribution curve, the effect of a catalyst on a chemical reaction.
breadcrumbLabels: 6.1 Collision theory and rates of reaction
